title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. pseudoconsociatiforme
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. pseudoconsociatiforme, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is also known for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Hieracium lachenalii subsp. pseudoconsociatiforme can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal and health benefits.
